title: Leontodon olivierii
taxonomy: Leontodon olivierii is a species within the genus Leontodon and is classified as a member of the family Asteraceae. This classification places it in the order Asterales. Further classification includes the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. It is a part of the larger clade called the angiosperms, or flowering plants.
uses: Leontodon olivierii, commonly known as the Mediterranean hawkbit, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and flowers are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. In some parts of Europe, it is used to treat digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of fever. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Leontodon olivierii for medicinal purposes.

parts: Leontodon olivierii, commonly known as the dune hawk's-beard, has various medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used in traditional medicine.
